title_en: 91. Be brave and start from now! (cont.)
content_en: My mother is like any other aged woman who loves to talk about womanly issues, such as who got married or divorced, how many sons so-and-so has, which of them is the eldest, what is the name of her пђБrst child, and so on. I consider all this frivolous, but she loves chatting about it. She feels that the information she shares with me is priceless as it cannot be found in any book, audio or website. She feels that she is relating unique information to me, and is delighted to do so! Whenever I sit with her I instigate such conversations and she becomes elated and continues to speak ceaselessly. My brothers talk about issues that do not interest her and so she feels bored in their company and longs to be in mine! That is all there is to it.вАЭ Yes, if you realise the nature of the one you are conversing Use the right пђВavour with, what he likes and dislikes, you will be able to capture his heart. Whoever looks at the way the Prophet пБ• dealt with people would realise that he would deal with everyone in accordance with their personality. Even when he would deal with his wives, he would do so on the basis of the personality of each. вАШAвАЩishah was very much of an extrovert and so the Prophet пБ• would often joke with her. Once she went with him on a journey and on the way back, as they approached Madinah, the Prophet пБ• said to the people, вАЬContinue on and leave us behind for a while.вАЭ The people carried on, leaving them behind, and so the Prophet пБ• was left with вАШAвАЩishah. At the time, вАШAвАЩishah was a young and energetic girl. The Prophet пБ• turned to her and said, вАЬCome, letвАЩs race!вАЭ She therefore raced him, and вАШAвАЩishah won. After some time, she went out again with the Prophet пБ• on another journey. By now she had grown and put on weight. The Prophet пБ• said to the people, вАЬCarry on...вАЭ and they did. He then said to вАШAвАЩishah, вАЬCome, letвАЩs race!вАЭ They raced, and this time, the Prophet пБ• won. The Prophet пБ• then started to joke about it as he patted her on her back, saying, вАЬThat was for last time when you defeated me! That was for last time when you defeated me!вАЭ He would, however, deal very differently with Khadijah, as she was пђБfteen years his senior. He would take this into consideration even when dealing with his companions. He would not deal with Abu Hurayrah пБі as he would with Khalid пБі, or with Abu Bakr пБі as he would with Talhah пБі. He would deal with вАШUmar in a way he would never do with anyone else. When the Prophet пБ• went out with his Companions to the Use the right пђВavour battle of Badr and came to know that the Quraysh were also on their way, he knew that some men from the Quraysh were coerced into going and had no intention of пђБghting the Muslims.He stood amongst his companions and said, вАЬI have come to know that some men from Banu Hashim and others have been forced to come. They do not want to пђБght us.Therefore, whoever of you meets anyone from Banu Hashim in the battle, let him not kill him. Whoever sees Abu al-Bakhtari bin Hashim bin al-Harith bin Asad, let him not kill him. Whoever sees al-вАШAbbas bin вАШAbd al-Muttalib, the uncle of the Messenger of Allah, let him not kill him, for he has been compelled.вАЭ It is also said that al-вАШAbbas was a Muslim who had concealed his faith and would inform the Prophet пБ• about QurayshвАЩs activities. The Prophet пБ• did not want him to be killed by the Muslims, nor did he want to expose the fact that al-вАШAbbas was a Muslim. This was the пђБrst battle ever to be fought between the two factions, the Muslims and the disbelievers of Quraysh. The Muslims were tense since they had not prepared for the пђБght and would soon be пђБghting their relatives, sons and fathers.Yet, the Prophet пБ• was forbidding them from killing certain people. вАШUtbah bin RabiвАШah was from the leaders of the Quraysh who were going to lead the battle against the Muslims, while his son, Abu Hudhayfah bin вАШUtbah bin RabiвАШah was with the Muslims. Abu Hudhayfah lost patience and said, вАЬShall we kill our fathers, our sons and our brothers and spare al-вАШAbbas?! By Allah, if I see him in the battleпђБeld, I shall strike him with my sword!вАЭ When his words reached the Messenger of Allah пБ•, he пБ• looked around and saw three hundred strong men. Yet, he turned Use the right пђВavour to вАШUmar exclusively and said, вАЬO Abu Hafs! Is the face of the uncle of AllahвАЩs Messenger to be struck with a sword?вАЭ вАШUmar said, вАЬBy Allah, this was the пђБrst time ever the Messenger of Allah пБ• gave me my nickname, Abu Hafs.вАЭ вАШUmar understood the message.
